---
title: "Analyzing Music Data"
author: "Harshil Kurapati"
format: html
execute:
  echo: false
---

```{r}
#| message: false
library(tidyverse)
```

```{r}
billboard |>
  select(artist, track, date.entered, wk1, wk2, wk3, wk4) |>
  slice_head(n = 8)
```
```{r}
summary(billboard$date.entered)
```

```{r}
ggplot(billboard, aes(x = wk6)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, boundary = 0, fill = "darkseagreen", color = "white") +
  labs(
    title = "Week 6 rankings only include songs still on the chart",
    subtitle = "Rank 1 is best; missing values are excluded (n = 280 songs)",
    x = "Week 6 rank",
    y = "Songs",
    caption = "Source: tidyr::billboard"
  )
```

```{r}
tibble(
  week = c("wk1", "wk4", "wk10", "wk20", "wk40", "wk76"),
  missing = c(
    sum(is.na(billboard$wk1)),
    sum(is.na(billboard$wk4)),
    sum(is.na(billboard$wk10)),
    sum(is.na(billboard$wk20)),
    sum(is.na(billboard$wk40)),
    sum(is.na(billboard$wk76))
  ),
  present = c(
    sum(!is.na(billboard$wk1)),
    sum(!is.na(billboard$wk4)),
    sum(!is.na(billboard$wk10)),
    sum(!is.na(billboard$wk20)),
    sum(!is.na(billboard$wk40)),
    sum(!is.na(billboard$wk76))
  )
)
```

```{r}
billboard |>
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("wk"),
               names_to = "week",
               values_to = "rank") |>
  group_by(artist, track) |>
  arrange(week) |>
  mutate(prev_na = lag(is.na(rank)),
         reentered = is.na(prev_na) == FALSE & prev_na == TRUE & !is.na(rank)) |>
  summarize(reentered = any(reentered), .groups = "drop") |>
  count(reentered, name = "songs")
```

```{r}
billboard |>
  select(artist, track, wk1, wk6) |>
  mutate(wk6_status = case_when(
    is.na(wk6) ~ "Missing by week 6",
    wk6 < wk1 ~ "Improved",
    wk6 == wk1 ~ "Same",
    wk6 > wk1 ~ "Worse"
  )) |>
  mutate(wk6_status = factor(wk6_status, levels = c("Improved", "Same", "Worse", "Missing by week 6"))) |>
  count(wk6_status, name = "songs")

billboard |>
  select(wk1, wk6) |>
  filter(!is.na(wk1), !is.na(wk6)) |>
  summarize(songs_with_both_weeks = n(),
            median_rank_change = median(wk6 - wk1))
```

```{r}
billboard_longer <- billboard |>
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("wk"),
               names_to = "week",
               names_prefix = "wk",
               names_transform = list(week = as.integer),
               values_to = "rank") |>
  mutate(song = paste(artist, track, sep = " - "))

billboard_longer |>
  filter(!is.na(rank)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = week, y = rank, group = song)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.18, color = "gray50") +
  scale_y_reverse() +
  labs(
    title = "Billboard rankings over time",
    subtitle = "Each line is one song after it entered the Hot 100",
    x = "Weeks since entering the chart",
    y = "Billboard rank",
    caption = "Source: tidyr::billboard"
  )
```

```{r}
billboard_top10 <- billboard_longer |>
  group_by(artist, track, song) |>
  summarize(
    first_rank = first(rank[!is.na(rank)]),
    best_rank = min(rank, na.rm = TRUE),
    first_best_week = min(week[rank == min(rank, na.rm = TRUE)], na.rm = TRUE),
    weeks_on_chart = sum(!is.na(rank)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  filter(best_rank <= 10)

billboard_top10_stories <- bind_rows(
  billboard_top10 |> filter(best_rank == 1) |> arrange(first_best_week) |> slice(1) |> mutate(story = "Fastest to #1"),
  billboard_top10 |> filter(best_rank == 1) |> arrange(desc(first_best_week)) |> slice(1) |> mutate(story = "Slowest to #1"),
  billboard_top10 |> arrange(desc(weeks_on_chart)) |> slice(1) |> mutate(story = "Longest run")
)

billboard_top10_stories |>
  select(story, artist, track, song, first_rank, best_rank, first_best_week, weeks_on_chart)
```

```{r}
#| cache: true
billboard_top10_longer <- billboard_longer |>
  semi_join(billboard_top10, by = c("artist", "track", "song"))

billboard_top10_longer |>
  filter(!is.na(rank)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = week, y = rank, group = song)) +
  geom_line(color = "gray75", alpha = 0.35) +
  geom_line(
    data = billboard_top10_longer |>
      semi_join(billboard_top10_stories, by = c("artist", "track", "song")) |>
      filter(!is.na(rank)),
    aes(color = song),
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = billboard_top10_stories,
    aes(x = first_best_week, y = best_rank, color = song),
    size = 2
  ) +
  scale_y_reverse() +
  labs(
    title = "Top-10 hits follow different chart paths",
    subtitle = "Each line is one song that reached the top 10 of the Billboard Hot 100",
    x = "Weeks since entering the chart",
    y = "Billboard rank",
    color = "Song",
    caption = "Source: tidyr::billboard"
  )
```

```{r}
#| message: false
music <- read_csv("data/music.csv")
music
```

```{r}
music |>
  select(starts_with("artist")) |>
  glimpse()
```

```{r}
music |>
  select(artist.name, artist.location, artist.latitude, artist.longitude,
         artist.terms, artist.familiarity, artist.hotttnesss,
         song.title, song.year) |>
  slice_head(n = 8)
```

```{r}
music |>
  summarize(
    rows = n(),
    artist_location_zero = sum(artist.location == 0),
    release_name_zero = sum(release.name == 0),
    song_title_zero = sum(song.title == 0),
    song_year_zero = sum(song.year == 0),
    familiarity_zero = sum(artist.familiarity == 0),
    hotttnesss_zero = sum(artist.hotttnesss == 0)
  )
```

```{r}
music |>
  summarize(
    total = n(),
    placeholder_coords = sum(artist.latitude == 0 & artist.longitude == 0),
    usable_coords = sum(!(artist.latitude == 0 & artist.longitude == 0))
  )
```

```{r}
#| cache: true
library(maps)
world <- map_data("world")

music_map_data <- music |>
  filter(!(artist.latitude == 0 & artist.longitude == 0))

ggplot() +
  geom_map(
    data = world, map = world,
    aes(map_id = region),
    fill = "gray90", color = "gray70", linewidth = 0.2
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = music_map_data,
    aes(x = artist.longitude, y = artist.latitude),
    alpha = 0.3, color = "steelblue", size = 0.6
  ) +
  expand_limits(x = world$long, y = world$lat) +
  labs(
    title = "Artist locations in the Million Song Dataset",
    subtitle = "Only 37% of artists have usable coordinates; US and Western Europe dominate",
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL,
    caption = "Source: Million Song Dataset teaching CSV"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text = element_blank(), axis.ticks = element_blank())
```


```{r}
#| cache: true
music_map_data |>
  filter(artist.familiarity > 0) |>
  ggplot() +
  geom_map(
    data = world, map = world,
    aes(map_id = region),
    fill = "gray90", color = "gray70", linewidth = 0.2
  ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(x = artist.longitude, y = artist.latitude, color = artist.familiarity),
    alpha = 0.5, size = 0.7
  ) +
  expand_limits(x = world$long, y = world$lat) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "plasma", name = "Familiarity") +
  labs(
    title = "Artist familiarity varies geographically",
    subtitle = "Higher familiarity clusters in the US, UK, and Western Europe",
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL,
    caption = "Source: Million Song Dataset teaching CSV"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text = element_blank(), axis.ticks = element_blank())
```

```{r}
#| cache: true
music_map_data |>
  filter(artist.hotttnesss > 0) |>
  ggplot() +
  geom_map(
    data = world, map = world,
    aes(map_id = region),
    fill = "gray90", color = "gray70", linewidth = 0.2
  ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(x = artist.longitude, y = artist.latitude, color = artist.hotttnesss),
    alpha = 0.5, size = 0.7
  ) +
  expand_limits(x = world$long, y = world$lat) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "plasma", name = "Hotness") +
  labs(
    title = "Artist hotness by location",
    subtitle = "Hotness clusters in the US and Western Europe, similar to familiarity",
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL,
    caption = "Source: Million Song Dataset teaching CSV"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text = element_blank(), axis.ticks = element_blank())
```

```{r}
#| cache: true
music_map_data |>
  mutate(genre = case_when(
    str_detect(artist.terms, "rock")  ~ "Rock",
    str_detect(artist.terms, "pop")   ~ "Pop",
    str_detect(artist.terms, "blues") ~ "Blues",
    str_detect(artist.terms, "jazz")  ~ "Jazz",
    str_detect(artist.terms, "metal") ~ "Metal"
  )) |>
  filter(!is.na(genre)) |>
  ggplot() +
  geom_map(
    data = world, map = world,
    aes(map_id = region),
    fill = "gray90", color = "gray70", linewidth = 0.2
  ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(x = artist.longitude, y = artist.latitude, color = genre),
    alpha = 0.5, size = 0.7
  ) +
  expand_limits(x = world$long, y = world$lat) +
  labs(
    title = "Geographic distribution of top 5 genres",
    subtitle = "Blues concentrates in the US; metal shows strong European presence",
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL,
    color = "Genre",
    caption = "Source: Million Song Dataset teaching CSV"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text = element_blank(), axis.ticks = element_blank())
```

```{r}
#| cache: true
music_map_data |>
  filter(song.year > 0) |>
  ggplot() +
  geom_map(
    data = world, map = world,
    aes(map_id = region),
    fill = "gray90", color = "gray70", linewidth = 0.2
  ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(x = artist.longitude, y = artist.latitude, color = song.year),
    alpha = 0.5, size = 0.7
  ) +
  expand_limits(x = world$long, y = world$lat) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "plasma", name = "Year") +
  labs(
    title = "Song release year by artist location",
    subtitle = "Older recordings concentrate in the US and UK; newer recordings spread more globally",
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL,
    caption = "Source: Million Song Dataset teaching CSV"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text = element_blank(), axis.ticks = element_blank())
```
